title_en: Hanafi Scholarship Across Centuries and Regions
title_id: Keilmuan Hanafi Lintas Abad dan Wilayah
content_en:
One of Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah's most valuable features is its documentation of Hanafi scholarship across the diverse regions where the school established itself, showing how the tradition adapted to different intellectual environments while maintaining its core legal methodology.

The Transoxianan Hanafi tradition — the tradition of Bukhara, Samarqand, and the surrounding regions — receives substantial coverage. This tradition developed its own characteristic features, including the major jurisprudential genre of the fatwa manuals (fatawa collections) that recorded the legal opinions of major scholars and became the practical reference for legal practice. Figures like Abu al-Layth as-Samarqandi (d. 373 AH), the Shams al-A'immah as-Sarakhsi (d. 490 AH), and others who developed the great systematic jurisprudential works are documented with the detail appropriate to their importance.

The Egyptian Hanafi tradition, in al-Qurashi's own context, receives careful attention. Egypt had a significant Hanafi scholarly presence despite the Shafi'i school's dominance, and Egyptian Hanafi scholars contributed to both jurisprudential writing and judicial practice. The biographies of Egyptian Hanafi scholars document an often overlooked dimension of Egyptian Islamic intellectual history.

The Anatolian and later Ottoman Hanafi tradition is documented in the earlier phases of the school's development in those regions. By al-Qurashi's time, the Ottoman state was still in its early stages, but the Hanafi tradition that would become the official Ottoman legal framework was already established in the Anatolian scholarly culture. Later Ottoman scholars added supplements to Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah to cover subsequent generations.

This geographic breadth gives Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah a panoramic quality among legal biographical dictionaries. Where some school-specific tabaqat focused on a single region or period, al-Qurashi attempted comprehensive coverage across all the regions where Hanafi scholarship flourished, reflecting the school's genuinely global reach. The variety of intellectual environments that Hanafi scholars navigated — from the rational theology of Mu'tazili-influenced Khorasan to the more traditionalist climate of late Abbasid Baghdad, and later the Ottoman institutional world — is documented in the biographical entries and gives readers an appreciation for how a single legal tradition can accommodate significant regional and methodological diversity while maintaining recognizable continuity.
content_id:
Salah satu fitur paling berharga dari Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah adalah dokumentasinya tentang keilmuan Hanafi di berbagai wilayah di mana mazhab mendirikan dirinya, menunjukkan bagaimana tradisi beradaptasi dengan lingkungan-lingkungan intelektual yang berbeda sambil mempertahankan metodologi hukum intinya.
Tradisi Hanafi Transoksiana - tradisi Bukhara, Samarkand, dan wilayah-wilayah sekitarnya - mendapatkan cakupan yang substansial. Tradisi ini mengembangkan fitur-fitur khasnya sendiri, termasuk genre yurisprudensi utama berupa manual fatwa (koleksi fatawa) yang mencatat pendapat-pendapat hukum ulama-ulama besar dan menjadi referensi praktis untuk praktik hukum. Tokoh-tokoh seperti Abu al-Laits as-Samarqandi (w. 373 H), Syams al-A'immah as-Sarakhsi (w. 490 H), dan lainnya yang mengembangkan karya-karya yurisprudensi sistematis yang agung didokumentasikan dengan detail yang sesuai dengan kepentingan mereka.
Tradisi Hanafi Mesir, dalam konteks al-Qurashi sendiri, mendapatkan perhatian yang cermat. Mesir memiliki kehadiran keilmuan Hanafi yang signifikan meskipun dominasi mazhab Syafi'i, dan para ulama Hanafi Mesir berkontribusi pada penulisan yurisprudensi maupun praktik kehakiman. Biografi-biografi ulama Hanafi Mesir mendokumentasikan dimensi sejarah intelektual Islam Mesir yang sering diabaikan.
Tradisi Hanafi Anatolia dan kemudian Ottoman didokumentasikan dalam fase-fase awal perkembangan mazhab di wilayah-wilayah tersebut. Pada masa al-Qurashi, negara Ottoman masih dalam tahap awalnya, tetapi tradisi Hanafi yang akan menjadi kerangka hukum resmi Ottoman sudah terbentuk dalam budaya keilmuan Anatolia. Para ulama Ottoman kemudian menambahkan suplemen-suplemen ke Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah untuk mencakup generasi-generasi berikutnya.
Keluasan geografis ini memberikan Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah kualitas panoramik di antara kamus-kamus biografi hukum. Di mana beberapa tabaqat spesifik-mazhab berfokus pada satu wilayah atau periode, al-Qurashi mencoba cakupan komprehensif di seluruh wilayah-wilayah di mana keilmuan Hanafi berkembang, mencerminkan jangkauan global mazhab yang sesungguhnya. Berbagai lingkungan intelektual yang para ulama Hanafi navigasi - dari teologi rasional Khorasan yang dipengaruhi Mu'tazilah hingga iklim yang lebih tradisionalis Baghdad Abbasiyah akhir, dan kemudian dunia institusional Ottoman - didokumentasikan dalam entri-entri biografi dan memberikan pembaca apresiasi tentang bagaimana satu tradisi hukum dapat mengakomodasi keberagaman regional dan metodologis yang signifikan sambil mempertahankan kesinambungan yang dapat dikenali.
content_ar: One of Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah's most valuable features is its documentation of الحنفي scholarship across the diverse regions where the school established itself, showing how the التراث adapted to different intellectual environments بينما maintaining its الأساسي legal methodology. The Transoxianan الحنفي التراث — the التراث of Bukhara, Samarqand, and the surrounding regions — receives substantial coverage. This التراث developed its own characteristic features, بما في ذلك the major jurisprudential genre of the الفتوى manuals (الفتاوى collections) that recorded the legal opinions of major العلماء and became the practical reference for legal practice. Figures like Abu al-Layth as-Samarqandi (d. 373 AH), the Shams al-A'immah as-Sarakhsi (d. 490 AH), and others who developed the great systematic jurisprudential works are documented with the detail appropriate to their importance. The Egyptian الحنفي التراث, in al-Qurashi's own context, receives careful attention. Egypt had a المهم الحنفي العلمي presence رغم the الشافعي school's dominance, and Egyptian الحنفي العلماء contributed to both jurisprudential writing and judicial practice. The biographies of Egyptian الحنفي العلماء document an often overlooked dimension of Egyptian Islamic intellectual history. The Anatolian and later Ottoman الحنفي التراث is documented in the earlier phases of the school's development in those regions. By al-Qurashi's time, the Ottoman state was still in its early stages, but the الحنفي التراث that would become the official Ottoman legal framework was already established in the Anatolian العلمي culture. Later Ottoman العلماء added supplements to Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah to cover subsequent generations. This geographic breadth gives Al-Jawahir al-Mudiyyah a panoramic quality among legal biographical dictionaries. Where some school-specific tabaqat focused on a single region or period, al-Qurashi attempted comprehensive coverage across all the regions where الحنفي scholarship flourished, reflecting the school's genuinely global reach. The variety of intellectual environments that الحنفي العلماء navigated — from the rational theology of Mu'tazili-influenced Khorasan to the more traditionalist climate of late Abbasid Baghdad, and later the Ottoman institutional الدنيا — is documented in the biographical entries and gives readers an appreciation for how a single legal التراث can accommodate المهم regional and methodological diversity بينما maintaining recognizable continuity.
